commands.review_summary:
The PR adds browser Notification API handling for incoming realtime messages outside the active visible conversation, updates embedded web assets, and adds Playwright coverage for that path.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR. The linked issue is source-reproducible on current main because realtime message handling exists but no browser notification path is present.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair is indicated because there is no definite code finding; the remaining blockers are contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of notification privacy scope.

target.title: feat(web): add browser notifications for incoming messages
target.body:
## Summary

Closes openclaw/clickclack#25.

This adds a narrow browser-notification path in the web app for incoming realtime `message.created` events:

- only runs when the browser `Notification` API exists and permission is already `granted`
- skips the current user's own messages
- skips the active conversation while the document is visible
- shows a small notification for messages arriving elsewhere
- focuses the app and navigates to the relevant channel or DM when clicked

Pushover remains unchanged. This intentionally does not add service-worker/offline push, a permission prompt UI, or `agent.progress`/busy-indicator behavior.

AI-assisted PR: yes. I understand the changed code path; this is wired into the existing realtime event handler and uses the existing channel/DM navigation helpers.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/chat.spec.ts -g "browser notifications announce"`
- `pnpm check`

Local Codex review was attempted with `codex review --base origin/main`, but the default model was unavailable to this project; retrying with `-c model="gpt-5.4"` hit quota. I did a manual final diff pass after the test/build gates.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  - Incoming realtime messages outside the active visible conversation now create a browser notification when browser notification permission is already granted.
- Real environment tested:
  - Local ClickClack app served by the repository Playwright web server on `http://127.0.0.1:18082`, with the real Go API, real realtime path, real web bundle, and dev-bootstrap data.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/chat.spec.ts -g "browser notifications announce"`
  - `pnpm check`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Focused e2e passed: `1 passed (6.8s)`.
  - The e2e creates a real channel, starts the app, sends a real remote message through `POST /api/channels/{id}/messages`, captures the web app's `Notification` constructor call, and invokes the notification click handler.
  - Full local gate passed: web build, SDK build, Go tests, root/workspace typechecks, oxlint, gofmt check, and oxfmt check.
- Observed result after fix:
  - The app created one notification titled for the target channel with the ClickClack icon.
  - Invoking the notification click handler navigated the app from `#general` to the channel that received the message.
- What was not tested:
  - Native OS notification rendering outside Playwright.
  - Requesting notification permission from the app UI.
  - Service-worker/offline Push API behavior after the browser is closed.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  - The automated proof fakes the browser `Notification` constructor so it can assert the app call deterministically in headless Chromium.
  - Current realtime `message.created` payloads do not include message body text, so notifications use a generic `New message` body unless future event payloads include a body.
target.author: pollybot13
